title: Community Engagement Demands, Tenant Association, Renovation Scope, and HCR Financing Timeline
summary: Council presses developers on community engagement -- initial meeting at church/Boys and Girls Club drew only a few attendees due to cold weather. Council insists on door-to-door engagement with both Parkside tenants and neighboring homeowners, not just scheduled meetings. Developers commit to monthly meetings with tenant association and broader community separately. Building details: Building A = 5 stories (former Patrician/Snowflakes Bakery site), Building B = 4 stories (former deli site). Current project funding separate from future east campus phase (future phase with NYS/HCR). West campus renovation scope: all-new kitchens (quartz countertops, wood cabinets), bathrooms, appliances, plumbing/electrical systems, roofs, windows, facade, landscaping, playgrounds, and electric heat pump HVAC replacing baseboard. HCR financing expected March-June 2026; renovation and new construction simultaneous. Commissioner Michael Collins explains HUD contract requires maintaining 393 affordable units -- east campus residents must be relocated to new buildings before any demolition. East campus problems detailed: super-block topography prevents emergency vehicle access, multiple unsecured entrances/exits, basement tunnels between buildings exploited for criminal escape, 70 security shifts required over summer due to site design.

department_refs: Department of Neighborhood and Business Development

speakers.name: Michael Collins
speakers.role: Commissioner for Neighborhood and Business Development

political_signal.description: Council demanding genuine community engagement comparable to East Adams, not just token meetings

processing_notes: HR, Fire, DPD in detected_departments are contextual false positives; actual department is Neighborhood and Business Development
HUD = federal Housing and Urban Development; HCR = NYS Homes and Community Renewal; LIHTC = Low-Income Housing Tax Credit
Building addresses: west campus = 1809, 1811, 1813, 1815, 1901, 1903; east campus = 2000-block
East campus tunnels = original mid-century design for maintenance/fire access, now exploited for criminal escape
Segment 4 of 7 from Economic Development Committee Meeting, January 22nd 2026
Potential duplicate detected: overlaps with CTX-COUNCIL-2026-00041 -- linked, not skipped
